growth_analysis.details: Revenue data incomplete; Flat or declining FCFF

risk_analysis.details: Beta NA; D/E 0.0; Interest coverage NA

macro_context.market_sentiment: Risk-on. S&P 500 and Nasdaq closed April at record highs — best monthly gain since 2020. May 1 futures edge higher (+0.2% S&P 500) though Q1 GDP came in at 2.0% vs 2.3% expected, and PCE inflation is running hot at 3.5%. Most global markets closed for Labour Day, reducing liquidity. Sources: TheStreet, CNBC, Yahoo Finance.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC held rates at 3.50–3.75% on April 29 in an unusual 8-4 vote (third consecutive hold). Powell's chairmanship ends May 15; Kevin Warsh advancing through Senate confirmation as successor. Three dissenters wanted to remove the easing bias; the committee retained it, keeping modest rate-cut optionality alive for H2 2026. Sources: CNBC, Al Jazeera, Fed.gov.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East conflict (Strait of Hormuz risk) driving energy price uncertainty; U.S. intervention in Venezuela; ongoing tariff disputes; critical semiconductor supply-chain tensions cited by IMF as growth headwinds. IMF projects global growth at 3.1% with inflation at 4.4%. Sources: IMF WEO April 2026, Lazard, Morgan Stanley.
key_events: AMD Q1 2026 earnings: May 5, 2026 after market close
Fed Chair Powell's term ends May 15; Kevin Warsh confirmation vote pending
Continued Middle East escalation risk affecting energy prices
US midterm election cycle beginning to influence policy uncertainty

ticker_news.headline: AMD stock hits all-time high of $353 on April 30, up 248% over trailing twelve months, driven by AI GPU ramp and data-center demand
DA Davidson upgrades AMD to Buy, raises price target to $375 from $220, citing data center GPU demand ahead of Q1 earnings
Intel blowout Q1 report (Data Center & AI revenue +22% YoY) seen as positive read-through for AMD EPYC server CPU business
Northland Capital Markets downgrades AMD to Market Perform from Outperform, cuts price target to $260, citing competitive headwinds
AMD Q1 2026 earnings due May 5 after close; analysts forecast $9.8B revenue (+32% YoY) and $1.30 EPS (+35% YoY)

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Strong net-positive revision cycle. DA Davidson upgraded to Buy, raised PT $220→$375. Susquehanna reiterated Buy, raised PT $300→$375. Melius Research reportedly targeting $380. Northland Capital offset with downgrade to Market Perform, PT $260. Net consensus: 34 analysts, 41% Strong Buy, 38% Buy, 21% Hold, 0% Sell. Source: MarketBeat, StockAnalysis, Benzinga (as of 2026-04-30).

web_context.competitor_activity: Intel surged +24.89% after blowout Q1 earnings (Data Center & AI revenue +22% to $5.05B) — the positive read-through supports AMD EPYC server CPU thesis. NVIDIA reported Q1 Data Center revenue >$30B with 75%+ gross margins, maintaining dominant AI GPU market share; AMD's MI300 GPU series is positioned as the cost-aware second-supplier alternative for hyperscalers. Custom silicon (Google TPU, Amazon Trainium) is emerging as a longer-term structural threat to both NVIDIA and AMD in AI training/inference workloads. Sources: 24/7 Wall St., IBTimes Australia, Motley Fool (April 2026).
web_context.earnings_info: AMD Q1 2026 earnings: May 5, 2026, after market close (5:00 PM ET). Consensus EPS estimate: $1.30 (+35.4% YoY). Revenue guidance: $9.8B ±$300M (+32.3% YoY). AMD Data Center segment expected to again be the key driver following record $5.38B in Q4 2025. Source: MarketBeat, Zacks, StockTitan (2026-05-01).
